description: Improves how teams deliver through clear standards, feedback loops, and adaptability.

signals: Follows established team processes and standards (DoR, DoD)
Keeps work items up to date and communicates progress
examples: Ensured stories met DoR/DoD before moving to production
Updated task status daily during a sprint

signals: Clarifies and enforces team processes and their benefits
Encourages participation in ceremonies and feedback loops
examples: Reminded the team to keep retros focused on actionable items
Documented trade-offs of a deployment policy for the team

signals: Challenges inefficient processes and advocates for quality practices
Raises concerns with data and suggests improvements
examples: Proposed adding end-to-end tests after observing defect trends
Highlighted vendor coordination gaps and suggested adjustments

signals: Adapts and refines processes based on feedback and metrics
Guides the team through changes balancing speed and quality
examples: Reduced required approvals after measuring impact on throughput
Introduced release health reviews to improve stability

signals: Designs processes suited to team maturity and scale
Aligns ways of working with lean/agile principles and clear SLAs
examples: Created a disaster recovery playbook and regular drills
Defined vendor SLAs and collaboration frameworks

signals: Adjusts processes across multiple teams to enable cross-team delivery
Introduces metrics that reveal system and organizational health
examples: Rolled out a shared change management process across two tribes
Standardized incident postmortems with measurable follow-ups

signals: Defines organization-wide processes that balance autonomy and alignment
Ensures feedback loops drive continuous improvement at scale
examples: Designed a company-wide engineering operating model with governance
Established a cross-org review rhythm that improved lead times
